pages.full_text:
Tuesday October 9th.

Pegasus Bridge Bénouville  6th June 1944
first bridge liberated. Originally built 1932.
Rebuilt 1994
photo_captions: Pegasus Bridge Bénouville  6th June 1944 first bridge liberated. Originally built 1932. Rebuilt 1994
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in black and white photograph of Pegasus Bridge with a handwritten caption below it. The heading 'Tuesday October 9th.' appears above the photo. No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. Year of diary entry estimated as around 2001 based on proportional position in volume (page 300 of 350, volume covers 1989-2003); the museum at Pegasus Bridge was noted as 'only built a couple of years ago' on the right page, and the bridge was 'Rebuilt 1994', suggesting visit mid-to-late 1990s or early 2000s.

pages.full_text:
During the night there were light houses & lighted marker buoys. lovely sound of the sea.

Oct 9th Tuesday.

As we left, the van got stuck on the wet grass & mud. Then we scraped one of the water brackets on the drive.

We drove through __Deauville__ - beautiful large old houses. We stopped at Villerville for coffee. I walked down to the sea across the sandy beach to collect shells. Simon fixed the water tank. Travelling west we reached __Pegasus Bridge__ & looked at the museum there before and after lunch. The museum was only built a couple of years ago. It was the start of the Allied offensive back into Europe on D Day. The bridge, & the neighbouring bridge across the R. Orne, had to be taken from the [?Grem] Germans intact.

[?Brest] Bréville was badly damaged, on the ridge.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '4' appears at top-right corner; this may be a section/trip page number rather than the overall diary page number. The text at the very top ('During the night there were light houses...') is a continuation from the previous spread. The word before 'Germans' appears to read '[?Grem]' — likely a crossed-out false start ('Grem' struck through) before 'Germans'. 'Brest' at the start of the final sentence appears struck through or overwritten, replaced with 'Bréville'. Year of visit uncertain; estimated ~2001 based on proportional volume position. The 'Rebuilt 1994' caption and the museum being 'only built a couple of years ago' could place this visit anywhere from ~1996 to ~2003; 2001 is a reasonable mid-estimate. Right-hand text is cut off at the right margin on several lines (e.g. 'beautiful', 'scraped', 'shells.', 'Travelling west', 'at'), consistent with text continuing beyond the visible scan area — these truncations have been noted with the visible portions only transcribed.
